texts.label: text
texts.orig: Just like winnowing makes the husk blow away while retaining the grains, similarly, sages let the virtues stay while discarding the vices.
texts.text: Just like winnowing makes the husk blow away while retaining the grains, similarly, sages let the virtues stay while discarding the vices.

texts.orig: —Kabir
texts.text: —Kabir

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Malli and his sister Valli are excited for their summer vacations. Their parents have planned a trip to visit their relatives and friends living across India. They always stay in touch with their relatives and friends. Do you also keep in touch with your loved ones?
texts.text: Malli and his sister Valli are excited for their summer vacations. Their parents have planned a trip to visit their relatives and friends living across India. They always stay in touch with their relatives and friends. Do you also keep in touch with your loved ones?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Their first halt is at their Nani's (maternal grandmother's) house in Haryana. It is surrounded by large fields. Malli and Valli are fascinated by the variety of grains piled up in the courtyard. Their Mami (maternal aunt) and Mama (maternal uncle), along with other community members, are busy separating small stones and husk from the grains with their hands.
texts.text: Their first halt is at their Nani's (maternal grandmother's) house in Haryana. It is surrounded by large fields. Malli and Valli are fascinated by the variety of grains piled up in the courtyard. Their Mami (maternal aunt) and Mama (maternal uncle), along with other community members, are busy separating small stones and husk from the grains with their hands.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Malli and Valli are curious to know why this is being done. Their Nani notices their curiosity and explains, "We are removing these stones so that the grains are fit for cooking."
texts.text: Malli and Valli are curious to know why this is being done. Their Nani notices their curiosity and explains, "We are removing these stones so that the grains are fit for cooking."

texts.label: text
texts.orig: They go around the house to see the fields and the cattle. To keep them busy, Nani gives them a challenge of handpicking small stones from grains while keeping their eyes closed.
texts.text: They go around the house to see the fields and the cattle. To keep them busy, Nani gives them a challenge of handpicking small stones from grains while keeping their eyes closed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The method of picking by hand from a mixture (when two or more substances are mixed) such as small stones and husk from wheat and rice is called handpicking. It is done on the basis of differences in size, colour and shape of the particles. If the particles to be removed are present in small quantities and can easily be picked by hand, handpicking -proves to be a convenient method.
texts.text: The method of picking by hand from a mixture (when two or more substances are mixed) such as small stones and husk from wheat and rice is called handpicking. It is done on the basis of differences in size, colour and shape of the particles. If the particles to be removed are present in small quantities and can easily be picked by hand, handpicking proves to be a convenient method.

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Fig. 9.1: Handpicking
texts.text: Fig. 9.1: Handpicking

texts.label: text
texts.orig: At lunch, Malli and Valli are served steaming hot vegetable pulao. While eating the pulao, Nani observes that Malli is separating whole black peppers from the pulao and is placing them aside on his plate (Fig. 9.1). Valli playfully teases, "Wow! This is a handpicking method, nice one!"
texts.text: At lunch, Malli and Valli are served steaming hot vegetable pulao. While eating the pulao, Nani observes that Malli is separating whole black peppers from the pulao and is placing them aside on his plate (Fig. 9.1). Valli playfully teases, "Wow! This is a handpicking method, nice one!"

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Nani tells them about the benefits of black pepper and encourages Malli to eat it.
texts.text: Nani tells them about the benefits of black pepper and encourages Malli to eat it.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Later in the day, their Mama takes them to the fields where they observe bundles of harvested wheat stalks lying in the fields. Some stalks are spread in the sun for drying. Both of them pick up a stalk each and notice numerous grains attached to it. A group of farmers are beating the stalks on a large wooden log. Curiously, Valli asks Mama, "Why are they doing this?" Mama explains, "They are beating the stalks to separate the grains" (Fig. 3 9.2). This process of separating grains
texts.text: Later in the day, their Mama takes them to the fields where they observe bundles of harvested wheat stalks lying in the fields. Some stalks are spread in the sun for drying. Both of them pick up a stalk each and notice numerous grains attached to it. A group of farmers are beating the stalks on a large wooden log. Curiously, Valli asks Mama, "Why are they doing this?" Mama explains, "They are beating the stalks to separate the grains" (Fig. 9.2). This process of separating grains

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Fig. 9.2: Threshing
texts.text: Fig. 9.2: Threshing

texts.label: text
texts.orig: from the stalks is known as threshing. Farmers work hard, yet they enjoy their work. From time to time, they sing folk songs while working.
texts.text: from the stalks is known as threshing. Farmers work hard, yet they enjoy their work. From time to time, they sing folk songs while working.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The separated grains get mixed with piles of husk.
texts.text: The separated grains get mixed with piles of husk.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Valli whispers to Malli, "Will the farmers handpick the grains to remove them from so much husk?" She wonders, "How much time will the farmers take to separate these?"
texts.text: Valli whispers to Malli, "Will the farmers handpick the grains to remove them from so much husk?" She wonders, "How much time will the farmers take to separate these?"

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Let us perform an activity to find out the answer to the questions raised by Valli.
texts.text: Let us perform an activity to find out the answer to the questions raised by Valli.

texts.text: How do you think farmers separate so many grains from husk?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Traditionally, a soop (bamboo tray) is used for separating heavier and lighter components of a mixture (Fig. 9.3).
texts.text: Traditionally, a soop (bamboo tray) is used for separating heavier and lighter components of a mixture (Fig. 9.3).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Next day, their Nana takes them to the fields to show this process at work.
texts.text: Next day, their Nana takes them to the fields to show this process at work.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Malli and Valli observe a farmer standing on a raised platform. The farmer is moving the bamboo tray which contains threshed wheat grains in the direction of air or wind (Fig. 9.4).
texts.text: Malli and Valli observe a farmer standing on a raised platform. The farmer is moving the bamboo tray which contains threshed . , . . wheat grains in the direction of air or wind (Fig. 9.4).

texts.label: text

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This method of separating heavier and lighter components of a mixture by wind or by blowing air is called winnowing. Have you seen any similar kind of activity being done at your home?
texts.text: This method of separating heavier and lighter components of a mixture by wind or by blowing air is called winnowing. Have you seen any similar kind of activity being done at your home?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Next day, Malli and Valli board a train to Ahmedabad to visit their father's friend Ghanshyam bhai. Before leaving, Valli requests her Mami to prepare meethi puri (sweet Indian bread made from wheat flour) for their journey.
texts.text: Next day, Malli and Valli board a train to Ahmedabad to visit their father's friend Ghanshyam bhai. Before leaving, Valli requests her Mami to prepare meethi puri (sweet Indian bread made from wheat flour) for their journey.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Valli: Shall I help you knead the wheat flour?
texts.text: Valli: Shall I help you knead the wheat flour?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Mami: To prepare a dish with flour, first we need to remove bran that may be present in the flour.
texts.text: Mami: To prepare a dish with flour, first we need to remove bran that may be present in the flour.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Valli:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: How do we do it?
texts.text: How do we do it?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Mami: We use a sieve for this purpose.
texts.text: Mami: We use a sieve for this purpose.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Sieving allows the fine flour particles to pass through the holes of the sieve as shown in Fig. 9.5. The bigger particles such as bran and small stones remain on the sieve.
texts.text: Sieving allows the fine flour particles to pass through the holes of the sieve as shown in Fig. 9.5. The bigger particles such as bran and small stones remain on the sieve.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Carefully observe a sieve. Are all the holes of the sieve the same size? Will sieving work if the holes of the _ sieve are larger than the substances? Is there any difference in the size between the particles that pass through the sieve and the particles that remain on the sieve? Sieving is used when components of a solid-solid mixture have different sizes.
texts.text: Carefully observe a sieve. Are all the holes of the sieve the same size? Will sieving work if the holes of the sieve are larger than the substances? Is there any difference in the size between the particles that pass through the sieve and the particles that remain on the sieve? Sieving is used when components of a solid-solid mixture have different sizes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Upon reaching Ahmedabad, they visit Sabarmati Ashram along with Ghanshyam bhai where they learn about the Namak Satyagrah (Dandi March).
texts.text: Upon reaching Ahmedabad, they visit Sabarmati Ashram along with Ghanshyam bhai where they learn about the Namak Satyagrah (Dandi March).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Malli asks, "Where is namak (common salt) obtained from?"
texts.text: Malli asks, "Where is namak (common salt) obtained from?"

texts.label: text
texts.orig: "From seawater", replies Ghanshyam bhai.
texts.text: "From seawater", replies Ghanshyam bhai.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Seawater is a mixture of salts and some other substances dissolved in water. To obtain salt, the seawater is kept in shallow pits and exposed to sunlight and air. In a few days, the water
texts.text: Seawater is a mixture of salts and some other substances dissolved in water. To obtain salt, the seawater is kept in shallow pits and exposed to sunlight and air. In a few days, the water

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Methods of Separation in Everyday Life
texts.text: Methods of Separation in Everyday Life

texts.label: text
texts.orig: evaporates completely, leaving behind the solid mixture (Fig. 9.6). Common salt is then obtained from this mixture by further purification.
texts.text: evaporates completely, leaving behind the solid mixture (Fig. 9.6). Common salt is then obtained from this mixture by further purification.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is now time for Malli and Valli to visit Dada and Dadi (paternal grandparents) residing in Puducherry, located in southern India. They are excited to meet their old neighbourhood friend, Balan. After reaching Puducherry, they start talking about the old times and do not realise that it is already evening, Dadi's tea time.
texts.text: It is now time for Malli and Valli to visit Dada and Dadi (paternal grandparents) residing in Puducherry, located in southern India. They are excited to meet their old neighbourhood friend, Balan. After reaching Puducherry, they start talking about the old times and do not realise that it is already evening, Dadi's tea time.

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Fig. 9.9: Decantation
texts.text: Fig. 9.9: Decantation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Oh! Decantation is also used in washing and cleaning of rice an Siplsgs,
texts.text: Oh! Decantation is also used in washing and cleaning of rice and pulses.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Dada: I shall prepare tea for you. Children: We will also help you.
texts.text: Dada: I shall prepare tea for you. Children: We will also help you.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As Dada makes the tea, he shares tips on how to make a cup of tea.
texts.text: As Dada makes the tea, he shares tips on how to make a cup of tea.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Balan: After preparing tea, how do you remove the tea leaves?
texts.text: Balan: After preparing tea, how do you remove the tea leaves?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Dada: Obviously, with a strainer. You know if we do not have a strainer, we can still remove most of the tea leaves.
texts.text: Dada: Obviously, with a strainer. You know if we do not have a strainer, we can still remove most of the tea leaves.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Valli: How?
texts.text: Valli: How?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Dada: Leave the sauce pan (vessel) containing tea undisturbed for some time and gently pour the tea in a cup (Fig. 9.9).
texts.text: Dada: Leave the sauce pan (vessel) containing tea undisturbed for some time and gently pour the tea in a cup (Fig. 9.9).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Valli: Oh Yes! And then the tea leaves will be settled at the bottom.
texts.text: Valli: Oh Yes! And then the tea leaves will be settled at the bottom.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The process of settling down of heavier insoluble componentat the bottom ofa liquidis calledsedimentation. When the water (liquid) is removed by tilting the vessel, the process is called decantation.
texts.text: The process of settling down of heavier insoluble componentat the bottom ofa liquidis called sedimentation. When the water (liquid) is removed by tilting the vessel, the process is called decantation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: i In the chapter 'Materials Around Us', you have studied that oil does not mix with water and forms a separate layer when left undisturbed for some time. Which ; method of separation would you use to separate oil and water?
texts.text: In the chapter 'Materials Around Us', you have studied that oil does not mix with water and forms a separate layer when left undisturbed for some time. Which ; method of separation would you use to separate oil and water?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Dada: But I can still get a few tea leaves in my mouth because decantation does not completely separate all the tea leaves from the tea.
texts.text: Dada: But I can still get a few tea leaves in my mouth because decantation does not completely separate all the tea leaves from the tea.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Balan: Oh! It means itis nota proper method ofseparation. Dada: Yes, you are right. The tea is ready now.
texts.text: Balan: Oh! It means itis nota proper method of separation. Dada: Yes, you are right. The tea is ready now.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Malli picks up the tea-strainer from the shelf and gives it to his Dada.
texts.text: Malli picks up the tea-strainer from the shelf and gives it to his Dada.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Dada: Let me pour the tea through this strainer. You can see all the tea leaves collected in the strainer.
texts.text: Dada: Let me pour the tea through this strainer. You can see all the tea leaves collected in the strainer.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This process of separating tea leaves from tea is called filtration.
texts.text: This process of separating tea leaves from tea is called filtration.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Balan asks Malli if he could use a tea strainer to filter muddy water. Let us try and find out.
texts.text: Balan asks Malli if he could use a tea strainer to filter muddy water. Let us try and find out.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Dada: Also, try to filter the muddy water through a piece of cloth and observe the difference.
texts.text: Dada: Also, try to filter the muddy water through a piece of cloth and observe the difference.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Dada: In a piece of cloth, there are very small holes or pores between the woven threads. These poresinthe cloth can be used as a filter. People in ancient times also followed this practice.
texts.text: Dada: In a piece of cloth, there are very small holes or pores between the woven threads. These poresinthe cloth can be used as a filter. People in ancient times also followed this practice.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: But if the water is still muddy, impurities can be separated using a filter with even smaller holes or pores. A filter paper is one such filter that has very fine pores in it.
texts.text: But if the water is still muddy, impurities can be separated using a filter with even smaller holes or pores. A filter paper is one such filter that has very fine pores in it.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Other than the filter paper, many materials such as cotton, charcoal, and sand can be used as filters. The Choice of filter depends upon the size of the particles of the materials to be removed.
texts.text: Other than the filter paper, many materials such as cotton, charcoal, and sand can be used as filters. The Choice of filter depends upon the size of the particles of the materials to be removed.

texts.orig: Activity 9.5: Let us design and create
texts.text: Activity 9.5: Let us design and create

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Valli goes for a nature walk with her Dadi and collects some water from a pond in a container. She observes some unwanted substances in it. Design and create a working model of water filter using low-cost materials.
texts.text: Valli goes for a nature walk with her Dadi and collects some water from a pond in a container. She observes some unwanted substances in it. Design and create a working model of water filter using low-cost materials.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Tea bags were initially made of soft cloth, like 8 silk, because it could hold the tea leaves and let water pass through. Silk was strong and did not fall apart when it touched hot water. Later, people started using gauze or muslin. Eventually they began using filter paper, which is what most tea bags are made oftoday.
texts.text: Tea bags were initially made of soft cloth, like 8 silk, because it could hold the tea leaves and let water pass through. Silk was strong and did not fall apart when it touched hot water. Later, people started using gauze or muslin. Eventually they began using filter paper, which is what most tea bags are made oftoday.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Malli and Valli go on a boat ride in a nearby river with their Dada and _ his friend, Otukkam. Otukkam is a fisherman. As they cast the fishing net, water drains out through the mesh. Valli recalls the filtration method she had learnt and realises that this method of catching the fish is somewhat similar.
texts.text: Malli and Valli go on a boat ride in a nearby river with their Dada and _ his friend, Otukkam. Otukkam is a fisherman. As they cast the fishing net, water drains out through the mesh. Valli recalls the filtration method she had learnt and realises that this method of catching the fish is somewhat similar.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Malli is taken aback when he sees plastic bags, broken bottles, a large fish with a straw stuck in its mouth, and food wrappers along with the other fish trapped in the net.
texts.text: Malli is taken aback when he sees plastic bags, broken bottles, a large fish with a straw stuck in its mouth, and food wrappers along with the other fish trapped in the net.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Let us raise awareness about the issues related to river and ocean pollution by composing a poem.
texts.text: Let us raise awareness about the issues related to river and ocean pollution by composing a poem.

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Fig. 9.12: Magnetic separation
texts.text: Fig. 9.12: Magnetic separation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The carpenter starts handpicking the iron nails. The children tell the carpenter to wait. They get a magnet from their Bua. They ask the carpenter to move the magnet through the sawdust. All the nails get attracted to it (Fig. 9.12). Which method of separation did the carpenter use? Recall the chapter 'Exploring Magnets'.
texts.text: The carpenter starts handpicking the iron nails. The children tell the carpenter to wait. They get a magnet from their Bua. They ask the carpenter to move the magnet through the sawdust. All the nails get attracted to it (Fig. 9.12). Which method of separation did the carpenter use? Recall the chapter 'Exploring Magnets'.

texts.orig: This activity helps in assessing your understanding as to why we separate substances.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ¢ Handpicking is used for separating solid materials on the basis of differences in size, colour and shape from a mixture.
texts.text: ¢ Handpicking is used for separating solid materials on the basis of differences in size, colour and shape from a mixture.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The process in which the stalks are beaten to separate grains from them is called threshing.
texts.text: The process in which the stalks are beaten to separate grains from them is called threshing.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The method of separating lighter husk from heavier grains by wind or blowing air is called winnowing.
texts.text: The method of separating lighter husk from heavier grains by wind or blowing air is called winnowing.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The process of separating solids from a mixture based on variations in particle size using a sieve is called sieving.
texts.text: The process of separating solids from a mixture based on variations in particle size using a sieve is called sieving.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Evaporation is the process in which a liquid gets converted into its vapour. It can be used to separate a solid dissolved in a liquid.
texts.text: Evaporation is the process in which a liquid gets converted into its vapour. It can be used to separate a solid dissolved in a liquid.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Theprocess of settling down ofheavier insoluble component at the bottom of a liquid is called sedimentation. When the liquid is removed by tilting the vessel, the process is called decantation.
texts.text: Theprocess of settling down ofheavier insoluble component at the bottom of a liquid is called sedimentation. When the liquid is removed by tilting the vessel, the process is called decantation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Filtrationcan be used to separate insoluble solid components from a liquid.
texts.text: Filtrationcan be used to separate insoluble solid components from a liquid.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Churning is used to extract butter from curd.
texts.text: Churning is used to extract butter from curd.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: @ Separation of magnetic and non-magnetic substances by using a magnet is called magnetic separation.
texts.text: @ Separation of magnetic and non-magnetic substances by using a magnet is called magnetic separation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10. Read the following story titled 'Intelligent Leela' and tick the most appropriate options. Provide a suitable title of your choice for the paragraph.
texts.text: Read the following story titled 'Intelligent Leela' and tick the most appropriate options. Provide a suitable title of your choice for the paragraph.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Leela was working in the farm with her father when she realised that they left their drinking water at home. Before her father felt thirsty/hungry, she went to the nearby pond to fetch some water/grains. After obtaining some water in the container, she noticed that the water was muddy and fit/unfit for drinking. To purify the water, she kept it for some time and then she filtered/ churned the muddy water using a piece of paper/muslin cloth. Leela, then, cooled/boiled the water for about 10 minutes in a covered pan. After cooling/boiling, she filtered/churned it again and made it fit/unfit for drinking. She served this water to her father while having food, who blessed her and appreciated her efforts.
texts.text: Leela was working in the farm with her father when she realised that they left their drinking water at home. Before her father felt thirsty/hungry, she went to the nearby pond to fetch some water/grains. After obtaining some water in the container, she noticed that the water was muddy and fit/unfit for drinking. To purify the water, she kept it for some time and then she filtered/ churned the muddy water using a piece of paper/muslin cloth. Leela, then, cooled/boiled the water for about 10 minutes in a covered pan. After cooling/boiling, she filtered/churned it again and made it fit/unfit for drinking. She served this water to her father while having food, who blessed her and appreciated her efforts.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Technological developments have resulted in developing of threshing machines called threshers. These machines are used for separating grains from the stalks and husk. They perform both the tasks of threshing and winnowing simultaneously.
texts.text: Technological developments have resulted in developing of threshing machines called threshers. These machines are used for separating grains from the stalks and husk. They perform both the tasks of threshing and winnowing simultaneously.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: What is Sabarmati Ashram famous for? Draw a poster showing Dandi March and discuss why it was organised.
texts.text: What is Sabarmati Ashram famous for? Draw a poster showing Dandi March and discuss why it was organised.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Find out about some water bodies in India that contain common salt. One such source is Sambhar Lake in Rajasthan.
texts.text: Find out about some water bodies in India that contain common salt. One such source is Sambhar Lake in Rajasthan.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the traditional Indian system of holistic health and medicine called Ayurveda, the herbs or parts of plants are prescribed as remedies. These ingredients like roots, leaves, flowers or seeds of various medicinal plants are often dried in the shade. This practice facilitates the evaporation of excess water, leaving behind the important part of the medicine.
texts.text: In the traditional Indian system of holistic health and medicine called Ayurveda, the herbs or parts of plants are prescribed as remedies. These ingredients like roots, leaves, flowers or seeds of various medicinal plants are often dried in the shade. This practice facilitates the evaporation of excess water, leaving behind the important part of the medicine.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Is there any method through which I can get back both salt and water?
texts.text: Is there any method through which I can get back both salt and water?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Nowadays, recyclers use magnets to separate iron articles from a heap of waste.
texts.text: Nowadays, recyclers use magnets to separate iron articles from a heap of waste.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In many industries, the waste material often contains scrap iron. This is separated from the heap of waste materials using magnets fitted to a crane.
texts.text: In many industries, the waste material often contains scrap iron. This is separated from the heap of waste materials using magnets fitted to a crane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The scrap iron can be recycled and reused.
texts.text: The scrap iron can be recycled and reused.
